name: Capstone (Multi-Architecture Disassembly Framework)

performance.notes: C library backend; extremely fast disassembly

agent_readiness.notes: Multi-architecture disassembler. from capstone import *. md = Cs(CS_ARCH_X86, CS_MODE_64). for i in md.disasm(code, 0x1000): print(i.mnemonic, i.op_str). Architectures: x86/64, ARM/A64, MIPS, PowerPC, SPARC, RISC-V, WASM, M68K. Instruction detail: i.id, i.groups, i.operands. Used in pwntools, angr, binary analysis tools. Pure Python fallback but C binding much faster. The standard disassembly library for security tools.

gotchas: Only disassembles — doesn't execute or analyze semantics (use angr for that)
C library install can be complex on some platforms
Instruction detail mode must be enabled explicitly
Python bindings lag behind C library releases
best_when: Building security tools requiring instruction-level analysis
Malware analysis — extract and decode instruction sequences
avoid_when: Full binary analysis with CFG and data flow (use angr)
When radare2/Ghidra provides sufficient interactive analysis
summary: Capstone is a multi-architecture disassembly framework. Cs(CS_ARCH_X86, CS_MODE_64).disasm(code, addr) yields instruction objects with mnemonic, op_str, operands. Supports x86/64, ARM, MIPS, RISC-V, WASM. C library backend for speed. Standard disassembly library used by pwntools, angr, and binary security tools. Only disassembles — use angr for symbolic execution or CFG analysis.
